title: Web Security Criteria
text: Web Security Criteria When developing a web application, you need to determine the security criteria the application must meet. Security criteria can vary greatly, depending on the application, the sensitivity of the data it handles, the nature of the industry and the organization itself, and the risks and consequences of security breaches. For example, a banking application will have different criteria than corporate web site that delivers product information. Military and police applications will require different safeguards than a social networking application. When establishing your security criteria, you should consider: Laws and regulations that may be applicable, such as privacy laws. Industry standards that your application processes must comply with. Business risks that you need to avoid, such as compromised safety of people or property, identity misuse, financial risks, loss of reputation. Determine which data needs to be protected and to what degree, where the data resides, and where it crosses boundaries (for example from server to browser, or from server to backend database). You can use the information and your security criteria in designing and implementing your web application. Related Links Architecture and Design Considerations for Web Security To understand the threats to web security and to help you prevent and counter these threats, you need to understand the processes and components that make up a web application, how the data flows through the application, and the trust boundaries.
